name: refactor
tagline: Deterministic multi-phase refactor workflow (LSP/AST-grep, test gating, rollback) with an optional multi-agent 'team mode'.
verdict: Solid workflow: coverage gating, rollback/abort rules, no secrets or network risk. Downsides: 755 lines (over ~500 budget), relies on undeclared custom tools (LSP, ast-grep, call_omo_agent, team_*) missing on plain installs, auto git-commits each step without confirmation, and team-mode writes agent config to disk beyond stated scope.

highlights.summary: Phase 5.3 'Commit Checkpoints' and the team-mode Phase 5.1-T both auto-run `git add`/`git commit` after each verified step with no explicit per-commit user confirmation gate; Phase 0 only asks for cla

The 'refactor' skill's Team Mode addendum expands scope into full multi-agent orchestration: writing `~/.omo/teams/refactor-squad/config.json` to disk, spawning/monitoring parallel subagent workers, a
No negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the file -- it never states when NOT to invoke this heavy 6-phase, multi-agent process (e.g. for a trivial one-line rename a lighter edit might suffice).
